concept: Clinical Finding / Health-Relevant Observation Produced by a Stated Method
description: A health-relevant observation produced by a stated method: a measurement, an image, an examination result. The finding carries its method, its conditions, and its uncertainty along with it, because a number that has shed its method is not yet evidence of anything. It can support, conflict with, or simply run alongside a person's own reported 'phamori' (symptom), and neither one silently outranks the other.
sound_symbolism: A light current of air lifts 'hi' into bright 'i'. At the stressed center, 'sa' narrows that breath to a hiss before opening onto broad 'a'. Final 'ro' turns the tongue inside a rounder chamber. From a fine opening, the sound spreads clearly and ends in motion.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'shia ha hisaro sirami.' (They record this clinical finding.) Name the observer, method, conditions, time, uncertainty, and source record where relevant. Distinct from 'siru' (marker), 'masue' (measure), 'phamori' (symptom), 'somaki' (test), and 'kithero' (diagnose).

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A finding word that retains source and method prevents an observation from silently becoming a diagnosis or overriding the person's own account.

semantic_domains.cognition: a method-bounded observation available for interpretation
semantic_domains.physical: an observation concerning bodily or health condition
